reduction: Ψ(x^μ, r, Ω) = Σ ψ_{l,n}(x^μ)·u_{l,n}(r)·Y_l(Ω); integrate over (r, Ω) → three channels
central_finding: F²(x, c) is the throat form factor, NOT a radial overlap (overlaps are kinematics-independent constants)

tests.name: T1_kk_reduction_basis
tests.description: The Sturm–Liouville radial modes (Dirichlet BCs from B3) form an orthonormal KK reduction basis. Symmetric-FD eigenvectors are orthonormal (Gram ≈ I); the spectrum matches the Chebyshev solver.

tests.name: T2_radial_channel_masses
tests.description: Radial channel of the reduction: the KK masses are the radial eigenfrequencies ω(l,n), the closure-ledger lepton/quark mass ladder. Discrete spectrum.

tests.name: T3_s3_channel_gauge_propagator
tests.description: S³ angular channel: Hopf charge c₁ = 1 (gauge coupling) and S³ Green function flat limit → Coulomb 1/(4π r) (Fourier transform 1/q², the photon propagator from PRs #45–#46).

tests.name: T4_throat_channel_F2
tests.description: Throat channel (r → R_MID pinch): the form factor F²(x, c) = K(x)²·Q(x, c). Distinct from the radial modes (it is the throat-pinch dynamics, PRs #39–#41).

tests.name: T5_F2_is_not_a_radial_overlap
tests.description: CENTRAL FINDING: radial overlap integrals ∫u_m u_n dr are kinematics-independent constants (orthonormality: δ_mn). F²(x, c) varies strongly with (x, c). Therefore F² is NOT a radial overlap — it is the throat-channel form factor. The naive 'F² from radial integration' is falsified; the reduction channels produce different physical objects.

tests.name: T6_shared_substrate_consistency
tests.description: The three reduction channels share one geometric substrate: R_MID (throat radius), the closure quantum 2π, and the spin structure T² = −I. This shared substrate is the bridge connecting the mass and amplitude sub-threads.

tests.name: T7_b5_assessment
tests.description: The 5D → 4D reduction factorizes into three consistent channels (mass / gauge+propagator / F²) sharing the substrate (R_MID, 2π, T²=−I). The mass and amplitude sub-threads are structurally connected. Residual: F² is the throat form factor, NOT a radial overlap, so a single master integral unifying masses and F² is not achieved. B5 reduced from 'unconstructed' to 'factorized framework + one residual (master integral)'.

three_channels.radial: r ∈ [R_MID, R_OUTER] → KK masses ω(l,n) [mass thread]
three_channels.throat: r → R_MID pinch → F²(x, c) form factor [vertex]

scaffold_status.B5: substantially reduced (3-channel factorization; residual = master integral)

verdict:
BRIDGE FACTORIZED. The 5D → 4D radial reduction of the Tangherlini bulk action factorizes into three consistent channels, all reductions of one action on the same internal geometry:

  radial  (r ∈ [R_MID, R_OUTER]) → KK masses ω(l,n)    [mass sub-thread]
  S³      (Ω ∈ S³)               → c₁ = 1 + 1/q²        [gauge + propagator]
  throat  (r → R_MID pinch)      → F²(x, c) form factor [vertex]

The radial modes form an orthonormal KK basis (Gram ≈ I) with masses matching the Chebyshev solver; the S³ channel gives the Hopf charge c₁ = 1 and the Coulomb/1/q² propagator; the throat channel gives F² = K²·Q. All three share the substrate: R_MID (throat radius), the closure quantum 2π, and the spin structure T² = −I (the radial Dirichlet BC and the throat transport are the same datum). The mass and amplitude sub-threads are thereby structurally connected.

CENTRAL FINDING (T5): F²(x, c) is NOT a radial overlap integral. Radial overlaps ∫u_m u_n dr are kinematics-independent constants (orthonormality δ_mn); F²(x, c) varies strongly with the scattering kinematics (x, c). So F² is the throat-channel form factor, not a KK overlap — the naive "F² from radial integration" is falsified. The reduction connects the sub-threads structurally (shared substrate, three channels of one action) but does NOT unify masses and F² into a single master integral.

B5 is substantially reduced: from "the reduction map is unconstructed" to "the reduction factorizes into three consistent channels, with one residual — a single master integral producing masses AND F² together." Scaffold status: B1, B2, B3 closed; B5 reduced to its residual; B4 (dimensional bridge / m_e anchor) remains.
